page: Amazon Web Services r8a.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz, supported by 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz. This configuration yields a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The CPU cache layout includes 48 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance runs on the Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the r8a.4xlarge demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, scoring in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core CPU tests, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip compression. Multi-core performance, measured by stress-ng and PassMark CPU scores, falls within the average range. Memory performance is strong, with PassMark memory scores in the top 25%, though uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidths remain in the average tier. The instance also shows top-tier efficiency in processing small language models and text processing, while medium-sized model inference performs at an average level.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8a.4xlarge offers a high-density memory profile relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs means users must rely on network-attached storage, which aligns with workloads prioritizing memory capacity over high-speed local disk I/O. This instance is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, static web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8a.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, providing a generous 8.0 GB of RAM per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps. Benchmark results highlight top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory scores, alongside average multi-core and database execution capabilities. This hardware profile makes the r8a.4xlarge highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, including database operations, static web serving, and small-scale language model inference, where high memory capacity and single-threaded efficiency are prioritized over local storage performance.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier single-core performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86 instance featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM and top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 physical cores operating at 4.5 GHz
128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for memory-intensive databases and static web serving
